chunk_title: 1 - Confirm shipping address in Your Orders
markdown:
# Find a Missing Package that Shows as Delivered



Follow these steps if tracking shows a package as delivered in Your Orders but you can't find it.



##### 1 - Confirm shipping address in Your Orders

##### 

Verify if your shipping address in Your Orders is correct. For more information on how to handle a wrong shipping address, go to Add and Manage Addresses.



##### 2 - Search for a notice of delivery attempt



* Check the Message Center.
* Search for a delivery confirmation in Your Orders.
* Check your mailbox or wherever else you receive mail.
* Search for a delivery photo.



##### 3 - Check around the delivery location



Check your delivery instructions in Your Addresses. The package could have been left where you requested.



##### 4 - Ask your household members and neighbors



Check if someone else accepted the delivery.



##### 5 - Wait 48 hours for the package to be delivered



Packages may be scanned as delivered before arrival.



##### 6 - You can check with the carrier



The carrier may have more information about the location of the package. To track your package on the carrier website or contact them, go to Carrier Contact Information.



##### What to do next?



If you have not located the package after following the steps above, you can:



If your order is shipped and sold by a third-party seller, contact the seller directly for assistance. Go to Contact a Third-Party Seller.

If your order was shipped by Amazon, contact Customer Service within 30 days of expected delivery. We'll do everything we can to help.

chunk_title: Check Your Push Notifications in the Amazon App
markdown:
# Push Notifications for Shipment Tracking



You can set up or edit push notifications to track your orders.



To receive push notifications for shipment tracking, ensure your phone settings allow app notifications and then turn on push notifications in the Amazon app.



##### Enable Push Notifications on Your Device



For iOS:



* Ensure you have the Amazon app (Go to the App Store and search Amazon)
* Go to Settings > Notifications > Amazon > Allow Notifications
* Turn on “Time Sensitive Notifications” and select all alert options for best notification of delivery



For Android:



* Ensure you have the Amazon app (Go to the App Store and search Amazon)
* Go to Settings > Notifications > App Notifications > Amazon > Turn on
* Turn on “Time Sensitive Notifications” and select all alert options for best notification of delivery



##### Check Your Push Notifications in the Amazon App



Go to the Amazon app > Your Account (Person icon in the bottom left corner of the app) > Settings (cog icon in the upper right hand corner of the app) > Notifications > Account and shipping. Turn on all for Push notifications.

# Late Deliveries



What to do if your order didn't arrive on the estimated delivery date.



On this page:



* What to do if your delivery is late
* Why some deliveries are delayed
* How we’ll contact you if a delivery is late
* Why wait for 48 hours
* If 48 hours have passed since your estimated delivery date
* Guaranteed deliveries



##### What to do if your delivery is late



If your Amazon delivery is late:



* Track the package for status updates.
* Check your email for carrier notifications.
* Allow 48 hours past the estimated delivery date for unexpected delays.



##### Why some deliveries are delayed



Common reasons for late deliveries include:



* Bad weather
* Heavy traffic
* High delivery demand during holidays
* Custom delays for international packages
* Operational problems like a vehicle breaking down



##### How we’ll contact you if a delivery is late



If Amazon delivers the order, we’ll update your tracking with real-time information about where your package is. We’ll email you when we know there’s a delay. You’ll also find all correspondence in the Message Center.



For orders delivered by a delivery carrier, we’ll still update your Amazon order tracker. Your carrier will have the latest information. Contact your carrier directly for shipping updates. You can also check their website, emails, or mobile app for tracking information.



##### Why wait for 48 hours



Most deliveries arrive within 48 hours. If you haven’t received an email or notification about the delay, wait 48 hours as it may nearly be delivered.



After 48 hours have passed, Customer Services will be able to help you.



##### If 48 hours have passed since your estimated delivery date



If there's still been no news after 48 hours:



* For orders shipped by Amazon, contact Customer Service within 30 days of the estimated delivery date.
* Contact the seller if a third-party seller shipped and sold your order.



##### Guaranteed deliveries



For late guaranteed deliveries, you're eligible to receive a delivery fee refund:



* Go to Your Orders and select your late order.
* Choose Problem with an order, then Shipping or delivery issues, and finally Shipment is late.
* If you have an item with a guaranteed delivery date that wasn't met, we'll provide a refund of the shipping fees.



##### Related Pages



* Track Your Package
* Undeliverable Packages
* Find a Missing Package That Shows As Delivered
* Carrier Contact Information
* Delivery Guarantees

Most items include at least one free return shipping option.

Fees may apply when:
- Selecting a paid shipping or drop-off option
- Returning heavy or bulky items
- Having an unusually high return rate

The fee amount varies depending on:
- Item type
- Shipping method
- Size and handling requirements

Customers may incur fees if they do not complete the return before the return-by date. Fee structure:
- 20% of the item price during the first 30 days after the return-by date
- 100% of the item price after that period

Damage fees may apply if returned items:
- Are damaged
- Are missing parts
- Are not in original condition
- Have removed tags
- Show obvious signs of use

Fees can be:
- Up to 50% of the item price
- 100% of the item price for Amazon Luxury items

A 100% restocking fee may apply for:
- Opened software
- Activated video games
- Used games missing parts
- Opened collectible cards
- Board games
- Figurine figurines with missing parts or packaging

Amazon Gift Card | 2–3 hours
Credit Card | 3–5 business days
Shop with Reward Points | up to 5 business days
Debit Card | up to 10 business days
Checking Account | up to 10 business days
EBT Card | up to 10 business days
Cash at Participating Locations | up to 10 business days
Prepaid Credit Card | up to 30 days depending on issuer
Promotional Certificate | no refund issued

Heavy or bulky items include products that:
- Weigh 50 pounds or more
- Exceed 59 inches on the longest side
- Exceed 130 inches in girth

These returns may:
- Incur variable return shipping fees
- Require specialty carrier pickup appointments
- Require home collection services

Additional seller fees may apply for third-party seller returns.

# Deliver to Multiple Addresses

With our multi-address delivery feature, you can buy multiple items in one order
and send them to different addresses.

To send items from a single order to multiple addresses:

1. Add the items you want to buy to your Shopping Cart.
2. Select Proceed to Checkout.
4. Follow the on-screen instructions.

If sending any of the items as gifts, you can choose gift wrapping and write a personal
gift message for each item.

# Replace a Missing Packing Slip

# Replace a Missing Packing Slip

If a packing slip is missing, you can print an invoice for your package.

To print an invoice:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. In the drop-down menu, choose the appropriate date range.
3. Scroll to find the order, and select View Invoice.
4. Select Print button, or your browser's print button, to print the invoice.

You don't need a packing slip to make a return. We don't require packing slips from
single-box deliveries. This reduces your packaging waste. If you need an order receipt,
you can print one from Your Account.

# Sign for an Order

# Sign for an Order

Some items require an adult signature at delivery.

If you aren't available to sign for your order, go to Your Orders to check if the item shipped.

- If the order hasn't shipped:
  1. Go to Change Your Order Information.
  2. Select a different shipping address where someone can sign for your order.
- If the order already shipped:
  1. Contact the carrier.
  2. Arrange to collect the shipment.

  **Note:** If you can't collect your order, you can refuse delivery and contact us.

# About Security Codes

# About Security Codes

To protect your account and sensitive information, some Amazon services require additional
verification through a security code.

**If you haven’t received your code:**

1. Verify your mobile number is correct.
   - In Your Account, visit
     **Login and Security** to confirm your number is correct.
2. Request a new code
   - Select Resend Code.
3. Check that Amazon messages (35213) aren’t blocked
   - On iOS:
     - Go to Settings > Apps > Messaging
     - Ensure SMS is enabled.
     - Navigate to Blocked Contacts and make sure 35213 isn’t listed.
     - Unblock the number if needed, then request the code again.
   - On Android:
     - Go to Apps > Settings > Apps.
     - Tap the three vertical dots and select Special Access.
     - Note: On some devices, this may appear under App list > Filter > Show system apps.
     - Select Use Premium Text Message Services and set to Always allow.
     - If you still don’t receive codes, contact your carrier to enable Short Code SMS.
4. Try again
   - Request a new code and enter it when prompted.

# Approve a Teen's Order

# Approve a Teen's Order

When a teen places an order, a parent can review and approve the order through Your Orders, by email, or by text.

**Note:** As of April 7, 2025, Amazon is no longer accepting new signups for the Teen program.
The information below applies only to existing Teen accounts.

If your teen's order is below your preset spending limit, you won’t be asked to approve
your teen’s order. You'll be notified of the purchase. You can change these settings
in the teen’s profile in Manage Your Household.

To approve a teen's order do one of the following:

- Go to Your Orders to see orders placed by your teen. In the Amazon app, Your Orders can be accessed through the main app menu. Approve or decline each order awaiting
  approval.
- When you receive a text from Amazon requesting approval, reply with "Y" to approve
  the order. This option is not available if there are multiple orders waiting for approval
  or if the order contains more than four items. Instead, the text message will direct
  you to Your Orders to approve the order.
- When you receive an email from Amazon requesting approval, select the approval link
  in the email to approve the order.

**Note:** After 48 hours, unapproved orders expire.

# Change Your Subscription Email Preferences

# Change Your Subscription Email Preferences

You can use the links below to set your subscription email preferences.

To change your preferences for a specific subscription option:

1. Select the appropriate link from the following list:
   - Your Amazon Prime Membership
   - Your Subscribe & Save Items
   - Communication Preferences Center (for deals, product recommendations, new releases, and more.)
   - Amazon Delivers Email Subscriptions
2. Review your current preferences and make any desired changes.

   It may take 1-5 business days for your new preferences to take effect.

# Change Your Account Settings

# Change Your Account Settings

You can change your phone number, email address, name, password, or other Amazon account
information.

To manage your account settings, visit

Login and Security

How to Change Your Account Settings :

1. In Your Account, visit Login and Security.
2. Next to the account information
   you'd like to update, select Edit.
3. Follow the on-screen
   instructions and select Save
   Changes.
4. After you've completed all your
   updates, select Done.

Reset Your Password:

If you've forgotten your password, visit Reset Your
Password.

Still Need Help? Try:

- Change Your Language Preference
- Change Your Order Information

# Change Your Language Preference

# Change Your Language Preference

You can easily choose your preferred language on some Amazon sites.

Select the relevant link below to access the content in your preferred language to
learn more:

- This content is available in English
- Este contenido está disponible en español

## English

To change your language preference:

1. Go to Language Settings or select the country flag on the top right of this page.
2. Select your preferred language.
3. Save your changes.

**Note:**

- Some website features or communications may not be available in your preferred language.
- If you previously selected a preferred language, but the website is displaying in
  another language, ensure that you are logged into the same Amazon website that you
  set your preferred language in, for example, Amazon.ca or Amazon.de.
- If you change the language in the desktop environment, this won’t reflect in the mobile/app
  environment and vice versa.
- We are continuously improving the language experience on Amazon.com so that we can
  offer all our customers website features, content, and communications in the language
  of their choice. If you encounter a problem that you cannot resolve yourself, you
  can contact our Spanish-speaking customer service team.

## Español

Para cambiar las preferencias de idioma:

1. Ve a Configuración de idioma o selecciona la bandera del país en la parte superior derecha de esta página.
2. Selecciona el idioma de tu preferencia.
3. Guarda los cambios.

**Note:**

- Es posible que algunas comunicaciones o funciones del sitio web no estén disponibles
  en tu idioma preferido.
- Si previamente seleccionaste un idioma de preferencia, pero el sitio web se muestra
  en otro, asegúrate de haber iniciado sesión en el mismo sitio web de Amazon en el
  que configuraste tu idioma preferido, por ejemplo, Amazon.ca, Amazon.de, etc.
- Si cambias el idioma en el equipo de escritorio, esto no se reflejará en el móvil/aplicación
  y viceversa.
- Estamos mejorando continuamente la experiencia de idioma en Amazon.com para ofrecer
  a todos nuestros clientes funciones del sitio web, contenido y comunicaciones en el
  idioma de su preferencia. Si encuentras un inconveniente que no puedas resolver por
  tu cuenta, puedes contactar a nuestro equipo de servicio al cliente en español.

# Change Your Password

# Change Your Password

You can change your Amazon password by selecting Login & Security in Your Account.

To update your password, go to

Login & Security

To change your password:

1. In Your Account, select Login & Security.
2. Next to Password, select Edit.
3. Follow the on-screen instructions and select Save changes.

If you've forgotten your password, go to Reset Your Password.

Still need help? Try:

- Change Your Account Settings
- Track Your Package
- Why Can't I Log into My Account?

chunk_title: Manage a 'Multiple accounts use the same email' Error
markdown:
# Manage a 'Multiple accounts use the same email' Error

# Manage a 'Multiple accounts use the same email' Error

If you get an error saying 'Multiple accounts use the same email address' when trying
to sign in to Amazon, follow these steps to troubleshoot.

Amazon no longer supports using the same email address to create or sign in to more
than one account. If you receive this error, you must either sign in to the default
account for your email, which is the one you signed into most recently, or change
the email for your secondary account.

You cannot use the same email address to create or sign in to more than one Amazon
account. To fix the issue, sign in to your main email account, the one you used last,
or update the email address for your secondary account.

**You received this error message and you don't recall having multiple Amazon accounts:**

This error is not an indication that your account security is compromised. It can
happen if:

1. You unintentionally made another Amazon account before, possibly when you got locked
   out and had to create another one.
2. Someone else may now own your old email address and used it to create an Amazon account.
   They don't have access to your Amazon account.

**You do not intend to keep using this secondary account:**

Select the
Sign in to default account button. Sign in using the password for your default account. If you've forgotten
your password, you can
reset your password. To delete your secondary account, first change the email address, and then sign
in. After you've signed in,
request closure of your Amazon account.

**To continue using this secondary account:**

Select the
Use this secondary account option. Follow the steps for changing your email address.

If you want to sign in to your secondary account without changing email, set it as
your default account. When prompted to change your email address, select
Or keep using [email] for this account.. You will be guided through the process of making your secondary account your default
account. Your previous default account will still exist. However, at sign in you will
be asked to change the email.

chunk_title: Manage an ‘E-mail address already in use’ Error
markdown:
# Manage an ‘E-mail address already in use’ Error

# Manage an ‘E-mail address already in use’ Error

If you see an 'E-mail address already in use' error when trying to use an email address
on an Amazon account, follow these troubleshooting steps.

The same email address can't be used for multiple Amazon accounts. An ‘E-mail address
already in use’ error message will be displayed if you attempt to use the same email
address on more than one Amazon account.

**If the error occurs when trying to create a new Amazon account:**

1. Attempt to sign into the Amazon account with your password. If you have forgotten
   your password, you can reset your password.
2. If you're still unable to sign in and you're certain you've never created an Amazon
   account using this address, try creating an account using:
   - another email address you own.
   - an email alias. Check with your email provider if they support creating an email alias
     with your email account.
   - your mobile phone number.

**If the error occurs when trying to update the email address on your Amazon account:**

1. You may already own an existing account with that email address.
2. To use that email address on another account, you need to release it from your existing
   account. To release the email address:
   1. Sign in to your existing account that already uses the email address. If you don't
      remember the password, reset your password.
   2. Change the email address on your existing account to a different email address you
      own.
   3. Sign out of your existing account.
   4. Sign into the current account and update the email address, to the email address you
      released from the existing account.

chunk_title: Recover Your Account after Two-Step Verification Fails
markdown:
# Recover Your Account after Two-Step Verification Fails

# Recover Your Account after Two-Step Verification Fails

If Two-Step Verification fails and you can't access your account, you can try to
recover your account.

To regain access to your account, you'll need to verify your identity by providing
a scan or photo of a government-issued identity document. To protect your account,
we're unable to make changes to your Two-Step Verification settings until your identity
is successfully verified. If you're able to sign in to your account, you'll have access
to make changes to your account.

Before you begin account recovery, try to sign in with a registered backup method
or from a trusted device.

If you still can't sign in, to recover your account:

1. Go to Two-Step Verification Account Recovery.
2. Follow the on-screen instructions to upload your identity document.

   Make sure that your name and address, and the issuing authority (for example, state
   or country) are visible.

   Cover, conceal, or remove sensitive information such as account numbers or identification
   numbers.

The verification process can take 1-2 days to complete. We'll email you once we disable
the Two-Step Verification and then you'll be able to sign in to your account with
your password. We may still require additional security steps during sign-in to protect
your account, such as providing a one-time code sent to your email or your primary
phone number added to your Amazon account.

chunk_title: Removing a Teen Login from an Amazon Household
markdown:
# Removing a Teen Login from an Amazon Household

# Removing a Teen Login from an Amazon Household

Teen Logins are available to teens ages 13-17 years. Teens (18+) are eligible for
their own regular Amazon account.

Any time after a teen reaches the age of 18, the adult or teen may remove the Teen
Login from the Amazon Household. A regular Amazon account receives access to the following:

- An independent shopping experience without the need for adult oversight
- Freedom to use personal payment methods
- Ability to choose Prime memberships

To manually remove the Teen Login:

1. Go to Manage Your Household.
2. Choose **Edit Profile** of the Teen Login.
3. Select **Remove from Household**.
4. On the pop-over box, select Remove teen to confirm the removal.

A former Teen Login no longer has access to the payment method or Prime benefits of
the Amazon household (if applicable), but does retain:

- The Amazon Household shipping address
- Order history
- Saved items
- Wishlist-Preferences

Any active orders placed using the Teen Login that have been approved will still be
fulfilled. Orders currently awaiting approval will be cancelled and the payment method
will not be charged.

**Note:**

- Any items in the Shopping Cart as well as the Gift Card balance carry to the regular
  Amazon account.
- Teens currently enrolled in higher education studies have the option to register for
  Prime Student when they transition their account. To learn more about Prime Student
  benefits, visit Join Prime Student.
- Amazon Household allows sharing of Prime benefits (if applicable) for two unique adult
  accounts. If there is currently only one adult in your Amazon Household, visit Add an Adult to an Amazon Household.

# Reset Your Password

# Reset Your Password

If you have forgotten your password, you can reset it by going through our password
assistance process on our website.

To reset your password, go to

Password
assistance

1. When prompted, enter the email address or mobile phone number associated with your
   Amazon account and select Continue. 

   We will send you an email or SMS (depending on the verification method you have chosen)
   containing a One Time Password (OTP), to authenticate your request.
2. Enter the OTP you received and select Continue.
3. Create a new password.

   As soon as you have created a new password, it's active. Your new password is for
   all Amazon-related accounts associated with the same email address.

chunk_title: Set Order Approvals for a Teen Login
markdown:
# Set Order Approvals for a Teen Login

# Set Order Approvals for a Teen Login

You can set order approvals for a teen login in Manage Your Household.

**Note:** As of April 7, 2025, Amazon is no longer accepting new signups for the Teen program.
The information below applies only to existing Teen accounts.

Parents can choose to skip the approval step and set pre-approved spending limits.
You can set order approvals in Manage Your Household.

To set order approvals for a teen login:

1. Go to your Amazon Household.
2. Select Edit Profile for a specific teen.
3. Select Change order approvals.

   Parents will receive itemized notifications for every order, and can cancel and return
   any item in accordance with Amazon's policies.

# Unsubscribe from Amazon Marketing

# Unsubscribe from Amazon Marketing

Set your communications preferences to stop Amazon from sending you marketing emails
and direct mail.

To unsubscribe from Amazon marketing emails and direct mail:

1. Go to the Communications Preference Center.
2. From the General Settings category, expand the Email subcategory and uncheck, Do not send me any marketing email for now.
3. Select Save changes.
4. Expand the Mail category and uncheck Get occasional marketing about products, services, deals, and recommendations in the
   mail.
5. Select Save changes.

**Note:** You can manage your other email subscriptions (subscription emails, and emails about
programs you are enrolled in) in Your Email Subscriptions.

# What is Two-Step Verification?

# What is Two-Step Verification?

Two-Step Verification is a feature that adds an extra layer of security to your account
log-in.

When you try to log in, 2-Step Verification sends you a unique security code. When
you sign up for 2-Step Verification, you can choose to receive the security code by
text message or authenticator app.

You need to enter both the code and your password to log in.

To enable 2-step verification, go to

Your Account

## To enable 2-step verification:

1. In Your Account, select Login & security.
2. Follow the on-screen instructions.

## To disable 2-step verification:

1. In Your Account, select Login & security.
2. Select Disable beside Two-Step Verification.

## Related links

Managing Your Account

Recover Your Account after Two-Step Verification Fails

chunk_title: Select Your Card Currency for Amazon Currency Converter
markdown:
# Select Your Card Currency for Amazon Currency Converter

# Select Your Card Currency for Amazon Currency Converter

If your order is eligible for Amazon Currency Converter, the option to pay in your
card currency will be displayed at checkout.

Amazon Currency Converter uses information, such as the country or region your card
was issued in, to determine your card currency. If you want to change your card currency, you can update it at the checkout:

**Desktop**

1. From the **Payment method** section at checkout, select **Change**.
2. From your list of payment methods, select your payment method of choice.
3. While on your payment method, select **Change card currency**.
4. Select **My card is in a different currency**.
5. Select the currency of your payment method from the list.
6. Click **Use this payment method** to continue.

**Mobile**

1. From the **Payment information** section at checkout, select **Payment method**.
2. From your list of payment methods, select your payment method of choice.
3. Select **Change card currency**.
4. Select **My card is in a different currency**.
5. Choose the currency of your payment method from the list.
6. Click **Continue**.

Once you select your card currency, Amazon Currency Converter automatically displays
your payment total in your card currency on the order summary page, or the product
detail page for Buy Now ordering, so you know how much you're paying before you pay.

**Note:**

- Amazon stores your selected currency to use on future eligible purchases on Amazon
  websites where Amazon Currency Converter is available.
- If you don't see the option to pay in your card currency at checkout, then your order
  or payment method is not eligible for Amazon Currency Converter. See Amazon Currency Converter Requirements or Amazon Currency Converter Supported Currencies for more information.

# Resolve a Declined Payment

# Resolve a Declined Payment

To protect your security and privacy, your bank can't provide Amazon with information
about why your payment was declined. Contact your bank directly to solve these payment
issues.

To retry a declined payment, go to .

Your Orders

To determine why your payment was declined, consider the following, and, if necessary,
contact your bank for more information:

- Have you exceeded your credit limit?
- Did you enter your credit card number, credit card expiration date, billing address,
  and phone number correctly in Your Account?
- Is your purchase outside of your normal spending range? Some banks will block transactions
  due to security concerns.
- Does your issuing bank have special policies regarding electronic or internet purchases?

To retry a declined payment:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. Do one of the following:
   - Try again with a different payment method, as follows:
     1. Select Change Payment Method next to the order you want to modify.
     2. Select another payment method from your account or submit a new card number and select
        Confirm.
     3. Select Retry Payment Method next to the order.
   - Retry with your current payment method by selecting Retry Payment Method next to the order.

| Descriptor | Type of Charge |
| --- | --- |
| AMZ\*Prime Shipping Club amzn.com/bill  AMAZON PRIME\*A1B2C3D4Eamzn.com/bill | Charges related to Amazon Prime |
| Amazon.com\*PMT SVC 866-749-7545  AMZ\*(Company Name - e.g., Build, Age of Learning, ABC Mouse, etc.)  amzn pmts (checkout)  amzn.com/pmts, Kickstarter | Charges related to an Amazon Pay order |
| Amazon.com  AMZN.COM/BILL  Amazon Bookstore  POS Amazon  Amazon Merchandise  AMAZON MKTPLACE PMTS  AMZN Mktp US \*A1B2C3D4E | Charges related to an Amazon.com purchase |
| Amazon Digital Svcs amzn.com/bill  Video on Demand | Charges related to an Amazon Digital Service:   - MP3s - Kindle books - App downloads - Video downloads - Software or game downloads - Kindle Special Offers Opt-out Fees |
| AmazonFresh  amzn.com/fresh | Charges related to an Amazon Fresh order |
| Amazon Retail LLC | Charges related to an Amazon Books purchase |

# Identifying a scam

# Identifying a scam

Learn to identify, prevent, and report scams.

You can visit the following Help pages for additional tips to identify a fake communication:

How to identify fake emails

How to identify fake text messages/SMS

How to identify fake phone calls

How to identify fake websites

## What is an impersonation scam?

Impersonation scams occur when a scammer pretends to be a trustworthy organization
or person in order to steal your money or personal information. Impersonation scams
are perpetrated in a wide variety of ways - by phone, email, text, or even by messaging
you on social media. Scammers aim to have you make a payment of some kind, share personal
information, or give them access to your computer. They do so by pretending to be
a well-known organization with whom you've likely engaged.

## How do I identify an impersonation scam?

Impersonation scams can be difficult to identify. Here are some red flags indicating
an impersonation scam:

1. **False urgency**. Scammers often try to create a sense of urgency to persuade you to do what they're
   asking.
2. **Asking for personal information**. Scammers may try to persuade you to provide personal information over the phone
   such as addresses, payment information, account login credentials, or financial information.
3. **Purchases outside of Amazon**. Scammers may try to ask you to send money outside of the Amazon website or mobile
   app through a third-party site or wire transfer.
4. **Payment via gift cards**. Scammers may request you purchase and exclusively pay with gift cards. They may
   ask for the gift card number, claim code, or PIN.
5. **Unexpected order**. You may receive notification of an order or delivery for an item you weren't expecting.

If you received correspondence regarding an order you didn't place, it likely wasn't
from Amazon. Visit Your Orders to review your order history or, to report a suspicious
message, visit

Report a scam

If you have received suspicious communication claiming to be from Amazon and you don't
have an account with us, report it at reportascam@amazon.com.

For more information on the most common scam tactics, visit Scam trends.

To learn more, visit Trustworthy Shopping at Amazon

# Report Suspicious Activity

# Report Suspicious Activity

Amazon is committed to providing a safe and trusted shopping experience. If you believe that any product, seller, or activity is suspicious, follow these steps
to report it. For order questions, go to Your Orders instead of submitting a report.


1. Log into your Amazon account on your desktop, mobile or Amazon Shopping app,
2. Navigate to the product page,
3. Select "Report an issue with this product or seller", and
4. Select "This product or content is illegal, unsafe or suspicious"or "I have an issue
   with a Seller" to report suspicious activity,
5. Follow the prompts to enter details about your issue and submit.

**Contact Us.** Please visit our Customer Service page and select "Report Something Suspicious". You can find this option under "Help with
something else". You will be offered a choice between reporting a suspicious phone
call, email or SMS received or reporting a suspicious product or seller. If you would
like to report a suspicious product or seller, select that option too, then speak
with a Customer Service agent via phone or chat.

**Report a suspicious phone call, message, or other scam.** Follow the instructions in Report a Scam to report suspicious emails, phone calls, text messages, unsolicited packages, or
gift card scams.

**Stolen goods.** The sale of stolen property is illegal and strictly prohibited in Amazon. If you
suspect that a product for sale on Amazon.com is stolen, contact Customer Service.
Use one of the methods on this page and immediately file a report with your local
law enforcement agency.

For California residents, report suspected stolen goods to the California Office of
the Attorney General at the following link: https://oag.ca.gov/bi/retail-theft/report.

**Note:** To learn more about how Amazon keeps customers safe from counterfeit, fraud, and other
forms of abuse, visit Amazon's Trustworthy Shopping site.

chunk_title: Verify Your Email for a New Account
markdown:
# Verify Your Email for a New Account

# Verify Your Email for a New Account

We may ask that you verify ownership of your email address before creating a new
account.

**Important:**

If you think you already have an account, we recommend using that account. If you're
having trouble signing in, select 'Forgot your password?' on the sign-in screen.

You will not be able to create a new account using an email address that’s already
associated with an Amazon account. If you attempt to create a new account using an
email address that is already associated with an existing Amazon account, please see
Manage an ‘Email address already in use’ Error.

To verify your email address when creating a new account:

1. Select the link in the verification email.

   This email is sent automatically after you attempt to create the new account.
2. Follow the on-screen instructions.

**Note:** If you don't receive our verification email, do the following:

- Confirm that you entered your email address correctly .
- Check if the email is in your spam or junk folder.

# Add a Shopping Profile

# Add a Shopping Profile

An adult shopping profile is automatically created when an account is opened. You can create
up to five additional profiles for one account.

You can add up to five shopping profiles to personalize your experience:

1. Go to the
   Accounts & Lists menu and select
   Manage Profiles.
2. Select
   Add Profile.
   - Amazon Profiles displays profiles already created in your account from other services
     like Prime Video, Alexa, or Amazon Family. Using the same profile for shopping allows better recommendations based on all your
     Amazon activities.
   - If the profile you want to use is listed, you can select it and start shopping. If
     not, proceed to the next step.
3. Select
   Create profile.
4. Give your new profile a name and optional photo. The name can't be the same as any
   other profiles within your account.
5. Select
   Continue.
   - Once you've added a new profile, you're automatically switched to this profile and
     ready to shop.
   - You can edit your profiles through the
     View option.

**Note:**

- You can have six active profiles (one automatically created for the account holder,
  plus five more). If you reach the limit, you can remove a profile anytime. Select
  Remove profile and follow the on-screen prompts.
- In certain jurisdictions, a user that is 13-17 years of age may be prompted to create
  a Teen profile when they log in to an existing Amazon account. Unless prompted, you
  can't create a teen shopping profile.

# Add, Delete, and Manage Addresses

# Add, Delete, and Manage Addresses

You can add, edit and delete addresses for future orders. You can also select a default
address to make it easier to place an order.

Need to manage your addresses? Visit

Your Addresses

How to add, edit, and delete your addresses for future orders:

1. Visit Your Addresses in Your Account.
2. Do one of the following:
   - To add a new address, select Add address. Enter the new address details. Add your delivery preferences and select Add address.
   - To edit an address, select Edit below the address. Change the address details and select Update address.
   - To delete an address, select Remove below the address. Select Yes to confirm. You can’t delete an address with pending Subscribe and Save orders. First,
     update the Subscribe and Save order with a different address.
   - To set a default shipping address, select Set as Default below the preferred address. Having a default address makes the checkout faster.
     You can change your default address anytime. Note that selecting a default shipping
     address won’t change your billing address.

You'll be asked to confirm your payment card number next time you place an order using
the new or edited address.

How to change your shipping address on open orders:

Updating an address in Your Account won't change the shipping address on any open
orders, or on your Wish List.

- For open orders, visit Change Your Order Information.
- For wish lists, visit Create Your List.

Still need help? Try:

- Tracking Your Package
- Find a Missing Package That Shows as Delivered
- More on Where’s My Stuff?

# Archived Orders

# Archived Orders

You can view previously archived orders in Your Orders.

To view previously archived orders:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. - Search for the relevant order, or
   - filter for the date range that the order was originally purchased.

**Maintaining separate order histories**

For multiple family members to have separate order histories, consider using Amazon Family to link accounts. Linked accounts share select Prime benefits, and digital content
using Family Library.

# Bereavement support

# Bereavement support

We're sorry for your loss. Our dedicated support team is here to help.

## Protecting account information

To ensure privacy and security, we can only share account details or take most actions
related to an account when the request comes from the authorized estate manager.

If you can't provide all the required documents for authorization, we can still assist
with:

- Terminating subscriptions
- Stopping recurring deliveries
- Deactivating the account

For support regarding these topics, please email
bereavement-support-cs@amazon.com. Be sure to include the death certificate and the email or phone number associated
with the account.

## You have access to the email address or phone number associated with the account

If you have access to the email address or phone number associated with the account,
you can:

1. Sign in to the account to manage or close it.
2. Reset the password using the Forgot password link.

   Further guidance can be found on our
   Reset your password Help page.

## You don't have access to the account

To request account information, close an account, or make changes without having
access, verification is required.
This verification ensures that we only act on requests from an authorized representative.

To verify your authority, we require the following:

- **A copy of the death certificate,**
- **Proof of authorization** showing you're the legal representative of the deceased's estate (such as a probate
  or executor documentation),
- **A valid photographic ID** (such as a passport, driver's license, or ID card) of the authorized person, and
- **The email address/phone number** linked to the Amazon account.

If you don't know the email address, we need additional information to help us locate
the account. This additional information could be, for example, a unique charge ID
or order number. Those unique numbers can be found on delivery packages, confirmation
emails, or payment statements.

Once you've gathered the required documentation:

- Email it to
  bereavement-support-cs@amazon.com.
- Our team will verify the documents and act on your request.

All documents will be securely deleted after validation.

# Cancel Items and Orders

# Cancel Items and Orders

You can cancel physical items or orders that haven't entered the shipping process
yet. This page does not include information about canceling Amazon Prime and other digital
subscriptions.

**About Cancelling an Item**

- You can cancel items Fulfilled by Amazon or shipped and sold by Amazon that haven't
  entered the shipping process.
- Orders sold and shipped by a third-party seller can typically be canceled within one
  business day.
- Once an order is in fulfillment, sellers are required to approve cancellation.
- To cancel an order that has not entered the shipping process, follow these steps:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. Find the order that you want to cancel and select View or edit order .
3. Select Cancel items
4. Check box of the item that you want to cancel from the order. To cancel the entire
   order, select all the items.
5. Choose a reason for the cancellation and select Request cancellation.

**Cancellation Confirmation**

After submitting the cancellation, we'll send a confirmation message to the email
address on your account. For a history of your cancelled orders, visit Your Orders under Canceled Orders.

**Orders That Can't be Canceled**

- If your order is shipped directly from Amazon and can't be changed, refuse or return
  the package using our Online Returns Center.
- If your order is shipped directly from a third-party seller and can't be changed,
  contact the seller. For more information on contacting the seller, go to Contact Third-Party Sellers.

# Change Your Order Information

# Change Your Order Information

You can update your order quantity, billing address, delivery address, payment method, and more on your orders that have not yet been dispatched by visiting
the Orders section in Your Account.

To change your order information:

1. Go to Your Orders
2. Select Order Details for the order that you want to change. Then select Change next to the details that you want to update.
3. Follow the on-screen instructions.

**Note:** If you are on a mobile device, you can make changes to your payment method, but not
to your address. You may find more options to change your order by visiting Amazon.com
via desktop.

Change an Order from a Third-Party Seller

To update the shipping address of an order from a third-party seller, go to Contact a Third-Party Seller. For information on how to cancel items or orders, go to Cancel Items or Orders.

Change an Amazon Fresh Delivery

To add items to an upcoming Amazon Fresh delivery, go to Modify your upcoming Amazon Fresh, Whole Foods Market, and Third Party Merchant Delivery.

Not what you're searching for? Try:

- Track Your Package
- Cancel an Order in the Amazon Shopping App
- Find a Missing Package That Shows As Delivered
- Resolve a Declined Payment
- Add to Delivery with Amazon Prime

# Change Your Default Payment Method

# Change Your Default Payment Method

You can select your default payment method in Your Payments.

Select or change your default payment method in

Your Wallet

**How to change your default payment method**

- Go to Your Wallet. If you already have a default payment method selected, it will be listed first.
- Select the payment method to set as the default. Select Edit.
- Select the box Set as default payment method. You may need to complete authentication to default
  your payment method.
- Select Save.
- If you have existing subscriptions, you may be prompted to update your account. Select
  **Update**.

**Not what you're searching for? Try:**

- Change Your Order Information
- Update Your Payment Method
- Resolve a Declined Payment
- Check the Status of Your Refund
- Unknown Amazon Payment Charges

# Check Your Gift Card Balance

# Check Your Gift Card Balance

You can check your Amazon.com Gift Card balance in Your Account.

When you apply an Amazon.com Gift Card to an order or add one to Your Account for future purchases, we store the available balance so you can check it at any time.

To find your Amazon.com Gift Card balance in Your Account:

1. Go to Your Account.
2. Select Gift cards and check the balance on any of your Amazon.com Gift Cards.

**Note:**

- Some physical gift cards bought from Amazon.com don't come with values printed on them. In this case, you can check the value by
  going to Your Orders. In Your Orders, find the gift card order, and compare the 16-digit serial number (located beside
  the gift card amount) in the order details to the serial number on the back of the
  gift card.
- You can check all changes to your balance in the transaction section of your Balance Page, including order IDs or serial numbers.
- Your Gift Card balance doesn’t include possible promotional certificates added to
  your account.
- Please check the Amazon Gift Card Terms and Conditions to make sure that your item is eligible, when your balance is not provided as payment
  method during checkout.

chunk_title: Check Your Credit and Benefit Balances
markdown:
# Check Your Credit and Benefit Balances

# Check Your Credit and Benefit Balances

You can find information on your promotions, credit, and benefit balances in
Your Account.

Enter the prerequisites here (optional).

To check your promotional credits and balance:

1. Go to
   Your Account.
2. Select **Your credit & benefit balances**.

You can also go directly to
Your credit & benefit balances.

**Note:** Data for credit activity is only available for 365 days.

# Manage Payment Methods

# Manage Payment Methods

Add or update your payment methods through Your Payments in Your Account.

To manage your payment methods:

1. In Your Account, select Your Payments.
2. Choose one of the following:
   - To add a payment method, select Add a payment method.
   - To edit a payment method, select the card you wish to edit. When the card opens,
     select Edit. You can update your billing address or update card details.
   - To remove a payment method, select the card you wish to remove. When the card opens,
     select Remove from wallet.
3. Follow the on-screen instructions.

You can also add a new credit/debit card to your account while placing an order through
the shopping cart; when on the Select a Payment Method page, choose Add a credit or debit card and enter your details. The new payment method will be saved to your account and
appear as a saved credit/debit card when you place your next Amazon order.

Updating a payment method in Your Payments will not change the payment method on open orders. To change the payment method for
an open order, go to Change Your Order Information.

**Note:** Certain Health Savings Accounts (HSA) cards cannot be registered as Flexible Spending
Accounts (FSA) or HSA cards with Amazon but can still be used if they're registered
as credit cards. If you register your HSA card as a credit card, you'll be responsible
for ensuring the card is used only for eligible products. HSA cards that are registered
as credit cards do not automatically restrict the types of products you can purchase
with the card. Purchasing ineligible products or services with the card may require
you to report them and pay additional taxes. Contact your plan administrator or employer
for more information.

- With Tap to Pay, you can add a contactless card to your wallet to pay for your order.
  Use the Amazon app with a compatible device for Tap to Pay transactions. There are
  no transaction limits. Select payment method at checkout for the Tap to Pay feature to appear.
- Instructions will advise you to tap your contactless payment card to your phone to
  complete the order. Once successful, you'll be notified.

**Note:** In some instances, card issuers may provide Amazon with updated payment card details
when reissuing cards. For metal cards, the NFC antenna for tapping may be located
towards the rear of the card.

chunk_title: Manage Third-Party Apps with Login with Amazon Data Access
markdown:
# Manage third-party apps & services with data access through Login with Amazon

# Manage third-party apps & services with data access through Login with Amazon

Login with Amazon allows you to easily and securely use your Amazon account to sign
in to and share selected data based on your consent with third-party websites or apps.
If you no longer want to allow third-party apps or services to access your data, you
can remove their connection to your Amazon account.

To remove third party access:

1. Make sure you're signed into the Amazon Account.
2. Go to Your Account > Manage your data > Manage apps & services with data access.
3. Select Remove next to the appor service whose connection you want to remove.

   **Note:** Even if you remove access, the service can still use the information you previously provided. If you no longer want to get
   emails from a website or app using Login with Amazon, contact the provider directly
   for their unsubscription procedure.

chunk_title: Manage Your Amazon Accounts on Mobile Devices
markdown:
# Manage Your Amazon Accounts on Mobile Devices

# Manage Your Amazon Accounts on Mobile Devices

You can add, remove, or switch between Amazon accounts using the Amazon shopping
app on your mobile device.

**Note:** Do not add your Amazon accounts to a device that is public, or shared with unknown
people, because anyone with access to the device will also have access to your Amazon
accounts. Any new Amazon accounts added to your mobile app inherit the app permissions,
such as camera roll access, or preferences, such as language, automatically. Updating
app settings on one Amazon account updates them for all.

To manage your Amazon account on a mobile device:

1. In your Amazon shopping app, go to Menu.
2. Go to Settings and select Switch Accounts.

When signing out on your device, you'll be signed out only on this device.

If you need to sign out from a device you no longer have access to, sign into your
Amazon account on a desktop or mobile browser, and visit the Manage Your Content and Devices page. Under the Devices tab, select your device in the list, and select Deregister.

# Manage Your Backup Payment Methods

# Manage Your Backup Payment Methods

Backup payment methods help us avoid payment delays. You can manage your backup payment
methods through Your Payments in Your Account.

To add or update a backup payment method, go to

Manage backup payment method

If there's a problem with your payment, we'll automatically charge a backup card from
Your Payments for eligible products and services to make sure we fulfill our delivery promises

To manage your backup payment methods:

1. Choose enabled or disabled. Note that disabling backup payment methods may cause delays
   if your payment method fails.
2. Review and update your payment methods. Uncheck any you don't want to use as backup
   payment methods.
3. Select Save.

To change your payment method after your back up payment method is charged for that
order:

1. Go to Customer Service.
2. Select the order where the backup payment method was used.
3. Click on Change payment method.
4. Select a different preferred payment method.
5. Click on Confirm modification.

**Note:** The refund may take 7-10 business days to return to your payment method.

**Not what you're searching for? Try:**

- Manage Payment Methods
- Resolve a Declined Payment
- Change Your Order Information

chunk_title: Request the Closure of Your Account and Deletion of Personal Information
markdown:
# Request the Closure of Your Account and the Deletion of Your Personal Information

# Request the Closure of Your Account and the Deletion of Your Personal Information

You can submit a request for us to close your Amazon account and delete your personal
information permanently.


Follow these steps to submit a request to close your Amazon account and delete your
personal information:

1. Go to
   Close Your Amazon Account.
2. Sign into the account you want to close.
3. Review the products and services associated with your account.
4. If you still wish to proceed, you can select a reason in the drop-down menu. Then
   select the box next to
   Yes, I want to permanently close my Amazon account and delete my data. To complete the closure, select
   Close my Account.
5. A confirmation notification will be sent to the email address associated with your
   account or via text message. You’ll need to reply within five days to verify your
   request.

## Consequences of Closing Your Account

- Once your account has been closed, all products and services accessed through your
  account will no longer be available to you, across any Amazon site globally. For example,
  submitting your account closure request through this website will also close your
  account on
  amazon.com,
  amazon.fr,
  amazon.com.mx, and all other global sites that use the same credentials to access services and
  products offered through those sites.
- Once your Amazon account is closed, you will not be able to access and manage any
  of your business accounts – such as Seller accounts, Brand Registry accounts, or Advertising
  accounts – as well as other products and services included in the closed accounts.
- Once your account is closed, it is no longer accessible to you and it cannot be restored.
  If you decide later that you want to start ordering from us again, or if you would
  like to use website features that require an account, you'll need to create a new
  account. To learn more, visit
  What Happens When I Close My Account?

## Multiple Accounts

- If you have multiple accounts, you need to follow the listed steps for each of the
  accounts. This ensures that we have the correct authority to take action on each account
  you wish to close.

## Additional Support

- If you are experiencing issues with logging into the account you wish to close, go
  to
  Why Can't I Log into My Account? for further assistance.
- If you do not have an account, or if you are an authorized agent under applicable
  state law,
  email us

chunk_title: Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App
markdown:
# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

You can sign out of your Amazon account using the following steps.

To sign out of your Amazon account in the Amazon shopping app on Android and iOS mobile
devices:

1. Open the app and select the hamburger button on the bottom right corner of the screen.
2. Scroll down to the bottom of the page.
3. Select Sign-out and confirm.

chunk_title: Sign out of Your Amazon Account on the Amazon Website
markdown:
# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

You can sign out of your Amazon account using the following steps.

To sign out of your Amazon account in the Amazon shopping app on Android and iOS mobile
devices:

1. Open the app and select the hamburger button on the bottom right corner of the screen.
2. Scroll down to the bottom of the page.
3. Select Sign-out and confirm.

# Use Login with Amazon

# Use Login with Amazon

Login with Amazon allows you to easily and securely use your Amazon account to sign
into and share selected data, based on your consent, with third-party websites or
apps.

How to Use Login With Amazon

1. Go to a website or app that offers Login with Amazon.
2. Select Login with Amazon. 

   If our system doesn't detect an authenticated Amazon account, the Amazon-hosted sign-in
   screen will appear.
3. Sign in with your preferred method.
4. You'll be directed back to the website or app as a registered user, or prompted to
   complete registration.

Security:

Third parties are companies or developers who aren't Amazon. Only share your data
with third parties you trust.

When you use Login with Amazon, you'll always get an Amazon sign-in page with an Amazon
web address. You won't have to give your Amazon password directly to any other website
or app.

Managing Third-Party Access:

The first time you sign in, a screen requesting your consent to allow the third-party
application specific access to your data will appear. If you'd like to allow this
access, select Allow.

You can remove third-party access at any time by visiting Manage Login with Amazon.

Still Need Help? Try:

- Reset Your Password
- Change Your Account Settings
- Track Your Package

# Use Switch Accounts

# Use Switch Accounts

Switch Accounts allows you to add and switch between Amazon accounts without having
to sign out and in.

**Important:** To prevent others from accessing your Amazon accounts, we recommend that you don't
use this feature on shared devices and computers.

To switch or add accounts in a browser:

1. In the Account & Lists menu, select Switch Accounts.
2. To add a new Amazon account to the browser, select Add account. Enter your account credentials and follow the instructions on the screen.
3. To switch between accounts, select the account you want from the Switch Accounts page.

chunk_title: What are Mobile Phone Number Accounts?
markdown:
# What Are Mobile Phone Number Accounts?

# What Are Mobile Phone Number Accounts?

Mobile phone number accounts are Amazon accounts linked to a mobile phone number
instead of an email address.

## Contents

- Create an Amazon account with a phone number
- Add an email address
- Get notifications
- Check for messages from third-party sellers

## Create an Amazon account with a phone number

Mobile phone number accounts can only be created through the Amazon Shopping app for
iOS and Android smartphones.

To create a mobile phone number account:

1. Go to the Amazon shopping app
2. Select create an account
3. Add your information and verify mobile phone number

You must then use your mobile phone number to sign in on all platform types, like
desktop and mobile browser.

## Add an email address

To add an email address and get notifications like order updates via email:

1. Go to Your Account
2. Select Login and Security
3. Add your email address

## Get notifications

If you don't have an email address linked to your Amazon account, you'll get notifications
and emails through the message center.

We also recommend to:

- Download the free Amazon shopping app to get real-time notifications about your order
  updates and when items go on sale.
- Enable push notifications, on your smartphone and tablet settings.
- Check your notification hub on the Amazon app, under the bell icon in your account.
  All your push notifications are stored in the Notification Hub.

## Check for messages from third-party sellers

Currently, we don't send push notifications for messages sent to you from third-party
sellers. You'll still get emails, but you'll need to check for them.

If you're worried about missing an email, add your email address under Login and Security.

Otherwise, you can check for messages from third-party sellers in the message center
inbox.

## Not what you're searching for? Try:

- Reset Your Password
- Tracking your package
- Change Your Account Settings

| Your customer profile | - Your reviews - Discussion posts - Returns - Refunds for orders |
| Order information | - Your order history - Your invoices |
| Prime Memberships | - All types across all Amazon websites |
| Your Amazon Web Services (AWS) account | - The resources in your account |
| Your Amazon Selling Partner account | - The data associated with that account and Seller Central - Login abilities to Seller Central - Can result in limited or no access to the Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) inventory,   payment transactions, previous/future orders, tax related documents linked with your   Selling Partner account |
| Your Amazon Associates Programs account |  |

| Your Amazon Flex account |  |
| Amazon Pay | - Viewing past orders and filing Amazon Pay A-to-z Guarantee claims - Purchases using Amazon Pay that have not been billed and closed before closing your   Amazon account may still be charged. The billing is based on the merchant’s shipment   timing. - Any subscriptions where Amazon Pay is the payment method will have to be updated with   a new payment method. It's your obligation to provide the subscription provider with   a new payment method for billing. |
| Gift Cards | - Your balance will no longer be available to you   - You can check your balance on the Gift Cards Balance and Ledger page. You won't be able to redeem any additional Amazon Gift Cards.   - Any Gift Cards that you have sent to other customers can still be redeemed on their     accounts.   - Any third-party gift cards purchased from Amazon will still be valid at those third-party     websites.   - If you have purchased gift cards on behalf of a Corporate incentive program, you must     unenroll from that Corporate Gift Card program with the Corporate account manager. |

| Your wholefoodsmarket.com profile | - Your preferences |
| Your IMBb and IMDbPro account | - Your accounts and associated account data - Your linked membership subscription - Your order history |
| Your Amazon Registry TLD account | - All domains (such as .bot or .moi) purchased through a third-party registrar - Any value-added services |
| Digital Content and Services | - Your Prime Video account including watchlist, recommendations, subscriptions, and videos (including   videos you purchased) - Your Amazon Music purchases, playlists, and content - Your Amazon Photos (previously Amazon Drive) account and all account content - Your Audible profile and membership(s), credits, and access to your Audible content (such as audiobooks   and podcasts) - Your Kindle content and subscriptions, and any notes or annotations created through your account |
| Your Kindle Direct Publishing account |  |
| Your Author Central account | - All related author's pages |
| Amazon Appstore | - Your purchases - Your available coins |
| Amazon devices | - Devices such as Echo, Tablets, E-Readers, and Fire TV, will no longer be registered   to your Amazon account - You won't have access to Amazon services on your device(s) unless you register the   device to another active account   - Information stored in Amazon applications on Fire tablets, such as saved contacts     or calendar events bookmarks in the Maps app, and any device backups   - You can download your contacts or calendar events using in-app download functionalities. |
| Alexa | - Your device - The Alexa app and services available through Alexa (for example skills, alarms, lists)   on your Alexa-enabled devices - Your voice recordings - Other Alexa content and data |

# Print an Invoice

# Print an Invoice

You can print an invoice for your order in Your Account as soon as your order is shipped.

To print an invoice:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. Select Invoice underneath the order number.
3. Select Print this page for your records on the top of the order summary.
4. Select your browser's print button.
